title: Corporate Counsel (Two-year Contract- Immediate Vacancy)
description:
We welcome and encourage all applicants to submit your application at the LEGO Careers Portal: https://www.lego.com/en-sg/careers/job/corporate-counsel-two-year-contract-immediate-vacancy-f586c3897f0f1001c9b8f5a501a40000

Are you passionate about providing legal advice to our indoor LEGO® play attractions?

Join the LEGO Group’s Legal, Compliance, and Risk (LCR) team and play an important role in protecting the LEGO® brand and products for builders of the future!

Play your part in our team succeeding

  • Reporting directly to the Legal Director APAC, you will serve as a key legal business partner for our LEGO/LEGOLAND Discovery Center attractions. This Corporate Counsel position is part of the wider China & APAC LCR team which supports our fast-paced, high-growth regional market groups.

  • This is an exciting in-house generalist role with a primary focus on supporting a broad range of legal matters related to our portfolio of LEGO/LEGOLAND Discovery Centres across the APAC region. Your role involves providing real-time legal solutions and frequent collaboration with other legal colleagues and relevant business teams to enable our attractions to deliver world-class experiences. The nature of providing legal support to the operation of attractions such as the LEGO/LEGOLAND Discovery Centres demands quick decision-making and rapid assessments, so agility is important.

  • Beyond the legal work required for the LEGO/LEGOLAND Discovery Centres, this role will also be expected to support legal matters as and when required for the broader APAC market group under the direction of the Legal Director APAC.

  • Strong partnering skills are also essential, as you will work directly with various business teams.

Core Responsibilities

  • Act as the primary legal counsel and point of contact for LEGO/LEGOLAND Discovery Centres in APAC. You will manage a broad range of commercial, regulatory, and operational legal matters while also promoting the LEGO Group’s compliance agenda.

  • Partner directly with business stakeholders and LCR colleagues. In some cases, you will also serve as the main point of contact for a task towards business stakeholders, and in others you will support colleagues within LCR including the APAC LCR team.

  • Build strong professional networks and effective working relationships across the organization to ensure global consistency, compliance and proactive risk mitigation.

  • Support senior APAC LCR lawyers in reviewing and drafting a variety of commercial matters including contracts and providing legal advice on general matters for the broader APAC Market Group

       The core areas of focus will include:

  • Day-to-day legal support to the attractions: Support legal matters related to the operation of the LEGO/LEGOLAND Discovery Centres in the APAC region, ensuring all activities comply with all applicable local legal requirements.

  • Commercial and Procurement Contracts: Draft, review, and negotiate a variety of commercial and procurement agreements.

  • Consumer Protection: Advise on compliance with regulations and best practices to ensure the highest standards of consumer safety and protection are maintained for the LEGO/LEGOLAND Discovery Centres in the APAC region.

  • Marketing Laws: Counsel on compliance with marketing regulations, including advertising, promotions, and communication strategies, with an emphasis on lawful engagement practices.

  • Privacy Laws: Advise on data privacy matters to safeguard customer information and company operations.

  • General Legal Advice: Provide comprehensive legal guidance and strategic advisory on a range of legal issues as they arise to ensure the company’s activities are conducted according to applicable local laws.

Do you have what it takes?

  • Educational & Professional Background: A Law Degree (LL.B., LL.M., or equivalent) from a reputable university, with a minimum of 8 years PQE. Experience in both a reputable law firm and an in-house role within a global company is highly preferred.

  • Legal Expertise: A solid generalist background with significant experience in corporate and commercial law. Strong experience in employment and labor law, including conducting investigations, is a strong advantage.

  • Technical Mastery: Highly proficient in drafting, reviewing, and negotiating a wide variety of complex commercial contracts.

  • Agility & Resilience: A self-motivated team player and critical thinker who can adapt to change and uncertainty. You are proactive and remain calm when reacting to urgent, high-priority business and legal requests.

  • Global Mindset: You enjoy working across diverse cultures and time zones, taking full ownership of your work and responsibilities while keeping stakeholders proactively informed.

  • Collaborative Spirit: Highly collaborative, with a curious mindset and a positive “can-do” approach to problem-solving.

  • Business Acumen: The ability to deeply understand business ambitions and strategies to provide commercially relevant legal advice. You demonstrate a strong willingness to learn and the discipline to follow through on all tasks.

  • Independent yet Integrated: Capable of working independently while functioning collaboratively as an integral part of the APAC LCR team under the direction of the Legal Director, APAC.

  • Communication: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills in English. Proficiency in other APAC regional languages is a plus.

Nice to have:

  • Thrives with working across functional areas and on multiple initiatives and projects.

  • The role will support tasks across the global organization. Some flexibility in working with other time zones is expected.

  • Interest in engaging in fun activities with the team, those sponsored by the company, or as part of our community engagement program.

Decision-Making Authority:

  • Making evaluations and giving advice to business and LCR stakeholders related to managing legal risk.

  • Legal, financial and commercial impact.

#LI-SG1

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect:

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

postedCompany.description:
About the LEGO Group

The LEGO Group offers unique and challenging career opportunities in a global organisation founded on fun, creativity and innovation. The name 'LEGO' is an abbreviation of the two Danish words "leg godt", meaning "play well". And that has been our guideline since 1932. Today, LEGO Group is the world’s largest manufacturer in construction toys and LEGO products are sold in more than 130 countries. Our head office is in Billund, Denmark, and we have subsidiaries and branches throughout the world.

The LEGO Group is an inspiring and rewarding place to work. Our culture is characterised by an informal atmosphere, an open dialogue, and respect for each other. So if you are a dedicated professional with an imaginative mind who wants to take play seriously, we can offer you the building bricks for a great career.

To learn about how you can build your LEGO future please visit www.jobs.lego.com

We believe in a People Promise - Succeed together

The role of the LEGO People Promise is to enable execution of the business strategy and build the long-term health of the company. It defines why people should choose and commit the best of themselves to the organisation, clarifies the "give" and the "get" of the employee “deal” and provides a common point of reference for employee management.

At the core of the People Promise are the four pillars defining the LEGO employees:


Purpose Driven
Experience the pride, commitment and shared sense of responsibility to deliver our mission

Systematic Creativity
Combine your experience and imagination to find the best solutions – now and in the future

Clutch Power
Feel part of a family and collaborate across the global LEGO community

Action Ability
Be accountable, deliver what you promise and unlock your talent in the best interest of the company
postedCompany.name: LEGO SINGAPORE PTE. LTD.
